bead.title: pre_claim_intake decoder rejects readiness_checks.verdict=bool; entire fizeau queue idle
bead.description:
PROBLEM
ddx work --watch's pre-claim intake decoder fails to unmarshal the readiness_checks response when verdict is a JSON bool. Every claim attempt produces the same warn-only rejection, so the worker logs a warning and skips. With 23 ready beads in the fizeau queue and one fizeau-scoped daemon, the entire queue is idle indefinitely.

OBSERVATION (Fizeau, 2026-05-18 05:27-05:39 UTC; about an hour after the previous wedge bug ddx-8f2e0ebf)
- Worker: PID 1527216, ddx work --watch in /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau, etime 01:22:40 at observation.
- Log: /Users/erik/Projects/fizeau/.ddx/agent-logs/agent-loop-1779077845655338391.jsonl
- Pattern every ~50s: resource.preflight (status ok) -> pre_claim_intake.start -> pre_claim_intake.warn -> loop.idle.
- Sample pre_claim_intake.warn payload:
  {
    "bead_id":"fizeau-5b2a8950",
    "decision":"warn",
    "decision_source":"pre_claim_intake",
    "detail":"decode result: json: cannot unmarshal bool into Go struct field .readiness_checks.verdict of type string",
    "fingerprint":"15155a1cd44e96daf53eabb14c8eb3ccd8b0e5271d881208cfcbbf2869899f4f",
    "outcome":"intake_error",
    "policy_mode":"warn-only",
    "reason":"system_unready",
    "rule_id":"pre_claim_intake.system_unready",
    "suggested_action":"check the readiness route or harness configuration and retry",
    "system_reason":"unavailable"
  }
- Identical fingerprint across multiple bead_ids confirms it is a schema mismatch, not a bead-specific issue.
- Queue state at observation: open=44, ready=23, dependency-waiting=19. None of the 23 ready beads got claimed in the prior 30 minutes; commit log empty for the same window.
- Other beads (fizeau-a15106ab, fizeau-90b85c8b) WERE successfully closed earlier in the night through some other path (perhaps a different worker version or a different execute-bead invocation). Suggests this decoder bug appeared mid-session OR only affects certain readiness routes.

WHY THIS IS A BUG
1. Schema drift between the readiness-check producer and the pre_claim_intake consumer. One emits verdict as bool, the other parses it as string. Either the producer or consumer changed without coordination.
2. warn-only policy_mode masks the failure: the worker keeps polling and looks alive (loop.idle every 50s) but never claims anything. Operator has no signal beyond tailing the agent-loop log.
3. The 'suggested_action' text ('check the readiness route or harness configuration and retry') is not actionable for the operator — the actual fault is in DDx's own decoder, not in the route or harness config.
4. Same-fingerprint repeat: 100% of attempts produce the same error code; the worker should escalate to FAIL after N consecutive identical warn-only fingerprints instead of looping silently.

PROPOSED FIX
A. Decoder tolerance: accept verdict as bool, string, or both; coerce to a canonical representation. Add a regression test against a payload with verdict=true/false and verdict="ready"/"not_ready".
B. Producer-side schema lock: pin the readiness_checks JSON schema in a shared place that both producer and consumer reference; CI gate that fails on drift.
C. Same-fingerprint escalation: when the pre_claim_intake produces the same warn fingerprint on N (default 5) consecutive attempts across distinct bead_ids, escalate to operator-attention with the fingerprint and a single example payload. Do not keep silently looping.
D. policy_mode='warn-only' must not mask a 100% failure rate. Add a per-fingerprint counter and a guard.
E. ddx work --watch should emit a top-level operator-visible message (stderr or status command) when its claim success rate over a rolling window falls below a threshold (e.g., 0% over 10 attempts) — silent idle on a full queue is a stuck state masquerading as healthy.

CROSS-REF
Adjacent bugs filed in this session: ddx-57c40485 (index.lock held minutes); ddx-8f2e0ebf (worker wedges on route resolution). All three share a theme: silent failures inside the worker that look healthy from the outside. The user is currently unable to make progress on the AR-2026-05-17 follow-up beads because of this.
bead.acceptance:
1. pre_claim_intake decoder accepts readiness_checks.verdict as JSON bool, JSON string, or absent; coerces to a documented canonical form. Unit test covers all three. Verified by a focused decoder test in the pre-claim intake package.
2. CI schema gate: the readiness_checks JSON schema is defined in a shared place; producer and consumer both consume it; CI fails on schema drift.
3. Same-fingerprint escalation: after N (default 5) consecutive warn outcomes with the same fingerprint, the worker emits an operator-attention event and stops looping silently. Tested by injecting a faulty readiness payload and asserting the escalation.
4. Operator-visible signal: ddx work --watch surfaces (via stderr or 'ddx bead status' or a new 'ddx work status' subcommand) a warning when claim success rate over a configurable rolling window (default 10 attempts) drops below a configurable threshold.
5. Documentation: ddx README names the pre-claim intake contract, the failure-class mapping (warn vs operator-attention vs hard error), and how operators should diagnose silent-idle.

events.body:
status=open
reason=Bead ddx-f9ddaa68 is too large for one atomic commit. Its 5 ACs mix decoder implementation (AC1), CI schema-gate infrastructure (AC2), worker-loop escalation (AC3), CLI observability (AC4), and documentation (AC5), and the description names five distinct feature-sized fixes (A-E across the pre-claim decoder, the drain loop, the work CLI, CI, and docs). This trips the Step-0 "too big" triggers: AC mixes implementation/CI/docs, and the description names multiple feature-sized pieces in unrelated subsystems. Decomposed into 5 atomic children (depth 1; parent is depth 0, split permitted). children: ddx-606fe854  AC1  pre-claim intake decoder: accept readiness_checks[].verdict as JSON bool, string, or absent (root-cause fix; no deps) ddx-d67f124e  AC3  ddx work: escalate to operator-attention after N consecutive identical pre-claim warn fingerprints (folds in proposed fix D, the warn-only 100%-failure guard; no deps) ddx-977e1a7f  AC4  ddx work --watch: surface low claim-success-rate over a rolling window (no deps) ddx-c5d485c9  AC2  Lock readiness_checks JSON schema in a shared location with a CI drift gate (depends on ddx-606fe854) ddx-757a3344  AC5  Document pre-claim intake contract, failure-class mapping, and silent-idle diagnosis in README (depends on ddx-606fe854, ddx-d67f124e, ddx-977e1a7f, ddx-c5d485c9) edges: Children linked to parent via the Parent field (repo decomposition convention; recovery_decompose.go:184-223), making ddx-f9ddaa68 an epic excluded from execution-ready. A child->parent dep edge was deliberately NOT used because DepAdd(id, dep) means "id depends on dep" (gaps_test.go:334) and a parent with open children never closes (diagnose.go:336), which would wedge every child. Inter-child blocking deps recorded: ddx-c5d485c9 depends on ddx-606fe854; ddx-757a3344 depends on all four others. notes: ddx-f9ddaa68 updated with --notes recording the child IDs. Parent lifecycle is orchestrator-owned.
suggested_action=retry with a smart agent

no_changes

Re-filing children for them would return no_changes. The parent remains too large because the THREE genuinely-unimplemented ACs mix unrelated subsystems and implementation/CI/docs: AC2 (CI schema-drift gate), AC4 (live claim-success-rate operator signal), and AC5 (operator docs). Each becomes one atomic, independently executable child with PROBLEM/ROOT-CAUSE(file:line)/PROPOSED-FIX/NON-SCOPE, a named Test* symbol, and `cd cli \u0026\u0026 go test`/`lefthook run pre-commit` gates. Child 1 (schema) additionally regression-locks the already-shipped AC1 decoder so producer/consumer cannot drift again — the exact failure mode in the parent incident. Child 2 is scoped to NOT duplicate the existing same-blocker idle escalation; it adds the complementary rate-based guard (proposed fix E). Child 3 is docs-only and depends on Children 1 and 2 so it can reference the shipped schema target and the --claim-rate-window/--claim-rate-threshold flag names; no child-\u003eparent dep edge is used (a parent with open children never closes). Parent non-scope, the FEAT-010/ADR-023 governing references, and cross-ref beads ddx-57c40485 / ddx-8f2e0ebf / ddx-df77e668 are preserved across the children."}
